note: Curated program library — verified against official sources Jul 2026. 'underwritten' = the engine models this path automatically on every deal. 'cataloged' = available tool for deal structuring; verify current terms via the linked source before relying on it.

programs.name: 485-x (ANNY) tax exemption
programs.terms: <100 units: 20% affordable @ ≤80% AMI wtd avg → 35-yr exemption (25 full + 10 at affordability %). 6–10 units outside Manhattan ≤12,500 SF: 10-yr full exemption, no affordability, 50% stabilized. 100+ units: 25% @ 80% AMI + wage floors ($35+/hr).

programs.name: City of Yes — Universal Affordability Preference (UAP)
programs.terms: +20% FAR in R6–R10 when bonus floor area is permanently affordable at ~60% AMI average. Stacks with 485-x.

programs.name: Section 8 HCV / VPS rents
programs.terms: Voucher payment standards can exceed market in BX/parts of QN. Engine models +12% uplift on market units. Verify current VPS by ZIP.

programs.name: ELLA / 4% LIHTC + tax-exempt bonds
programs.terms: All-affordable: HDC bonds + 4% LIHTC equity + city subsidy (~$130k/unit modeled). Profit = developer fee (~10% TDC). Competitive award.

programs.name: HPD Mix & Match (Mixed Income Program)
programs.terms: 50/50-style mixed-income rental: ~40-60% of units low-income (avg ≤60% AMI), rest moderate/middle. HPD subsidy loan + HDC bonds; pairs with 4% LIHTC.

programs.name: HPD SARA (Senior Affordable Rental)
programs.terms: Senior (62+) housing, ≤60% AMI, subsidy historically ~$75–235k/unit + Section 8 SPRAC/PBV support.

programs.name: HPD Open Door / NIHOP-NCP (new homeownership)
programs.terms: Subsidized new-construction coops/condos/1-4 family for moderate/middle-income buyers — the exit for small infill lots if rental doesn't pencil.

programs.name: 467-m (office→residential conversion exemption)
programs.terms: Up to 90% tax exemption for commercial-to-residential conversions with 25% affordable (avg ≤80% AMI); strongest in Manhattan south of 96th.

programs.name: Article XI (HDFC) tax exemption
programs.terms: Discretionary full/partial exemption up to 40 yrs for HDFC-owned affordable projects — Council approval; often the lever that closes preservation and infill deals.

programs.name: 9% LIHTC (HPD QAP)
programs.terms: Competitive annual allocation, equity ≈ 70% of qualified basis for deeply affordable projects. NYC has its own QAP.

programs.name: NYS Brownfield Cleanup Program credits
programs.terms: Refundable state credits on cleanup + development costs for enrolled contaminated sites — pairs well with outer-borough industrial-adjacent parcels.

programs.name: HCR Unified Funding (4%/9% LIHTC, SLIHC, HTF)
programs.terms: State's main pipeline: annual RFP combining 9% LIHTC, state SLIHC credit, Housing Trust Fund subsidy. ~$270M/yr statewide. 4% bond deals roll year-round via HFA.

programs.name: Pro-Housing Community designation
programs.terms: Municipal designation is now a PREREQUISITE for most discretionary state funds ($100M+ pool). Check each town's status — it tells you where the state wants you to build.

programs.name: IDA PILOT agreements (county/city IDAs)
programs.terms: Westchester County IDA, Yonkers IDA, Dutchess & Putnam IDAs negotiate multi-decade PILOTs + sales/mortgage tax exemptions on multifamily — the suburban equivalent of 485-x, deal-by-deal.

programs.name: Westchester County housing funds (HIF / New Homes Land Acquisition)
programs.terms: County capital funds for affordable development and land acquisition in Westchester municipalities.

programs.name: HCR SHOP / supportive pipelines
programs.terms: Supportive housing capital + operating (ESSHI) — deepest subsidy tier if you incorporate supportive units.

regions.region: Connecticut (Greenwich · Westport · Ridgefield · New Canaan · Darien · Stamford · New Haven)

programs.name: CGS 8-30g — Affordable Housing Appeals Act
programs.terms: THE lever in wealthy CT towns: if <10% of a town's stock is affordable (true for Greenwich, Westport, Ridgefield, New Canaan, Darien), a 30%-affordable set-aside project can OVERRIDE local zoning denial — burden of proof flips to the town. Stamford/New Haven generally exempt (over threshold or moratoria — verify current status).

programs.name: CHFA financing + 4%/9% LIHTC
programs.terms: CT's HFA: tax-exempt bonds, 4%/9% LIHTC allocation, construction-perm loans. Joint CHFA/DOH funding rounds (~10 projects, ~500 units per round in 2026).

programs.name: DOH FLEX gap funding
programs.terms: State Department of Housing flexible gap subsidy paired with CHFA rounds.

programs.name: Build For CT (middle-income)
programs.terms: CHFA program funding middle-income ('workforce') rental units — fits the Stamford/New Haven market tier.

programs.name: Municipal abatements (CGS 8-215) + TOD grants
programs.terms: Town-option tax abatements for affordable housing; CT Communities Challenge / TOD grants for station-area projects (Stamford, New Haven).

regions.region: Northern NJ (Fort Lee · Englewood · Edgewater · Bergen)

programs.name: Long-Term Tax Exemption PILOT (N.J.S.A. 40A:20)
programs.terms: Municipal PILOT up to 30-35 yrs for redevelopment-area projects via urban renewal entity — NJ's workhorse; payment often ~10-15% of gross revenue instead of full taxes. Requires 'Area in Need of Redevelopment' designation.

programs.name: 5-Year Abatement (N.J.S.A. 40A:21)
programs.terms: Short-form abatement/exemption for improvements — simpler, town-option, good for smaller infill.

programs.name: NJEDA Aspire 3.0
programs.terms: Gap-financing tax credits for residential projects in incentive areas (can reach 60%+ of eligible costs in qualified locations; $500M+ annual capacity). Updated program rules in 2026.

programs.name: NJHMFA 4%/9% LIHTC + State Tax Credit Subsidy Program
programs.terms: Standard LIHTC pipeline plus NJ's new state tax-credit sale program (up to $500M) funding affordable/workforce housing.

programs.name: Mount Laurel / affordable obligations
programs.terms: Fourth-round municipal affordable obligations (2025-2035) force towns to zone for affordable units — builder's-remedy-style leverage in under-compliant Bergen towns.

programs.name: LIHTC 4% / 9%
programs.terms: The backbone of every affordable stack nationally; allocated via each state agency above.

programs.name: Opportunity Zones (OZ 2.0)
programs.terms: Permanent renewal (2025 law): capital-gains deferral/step-up for equity in designated census tracts — check tract maps for South Bronx, New Haven, Englewood sites.

programs.name: HUD 221(d)(4) / 223(f)
programs.terms: FHA-insured construction-perm (up to ~85-87% LTC, 40-yr amortization, non-recourse) — the institutional alternative to bank construction debt once you're at scale.
